message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Semaphore::acquire_owned_with_cx observes pre-cancel (tick 427)

Owned-permit companion to tick 421's borrow-variant
Semaphore::acquire_with_cx cancel-observation test. The owned variant
takes `self: Arc<Self>` and returns OwnedSemaphorePermit instead of
SemaphorePermit<'_>, used by call sites that need to pass a permit
across an await boundary or into a spawned task. The cancel contract
is the same — pre-cancelled cx on a zero-permit semaphore must yield
Err(AcquireError::Cancelled) promptly rather than block indefinitely.

Setup mirrors tick 421:

1. Construct Arc::new(Semaphore::new(0)) — fully contended, no permit
   will ever be available without external action.
2. Pre-cancel cx via cx.cancel_with(User, ...) before the call.
3. Wrap sem.clone().acquire_owned_with_cx(&cx) in a 2s outer
   timeout_with_cx safety net to bound the assertion window.
4. Assert elapsed < 1s AND matches!(inner, Err(AcquireError::Cancelled))
   specifically — Closed / PolledAfterCompletion would indicate the
   cancel didn't propagate through the owned acquire path.

Pinning both the borrow and owned variants together guards against a
regression where only one path picks up a future cx-cancel-
observability change in asupersync. They delegate to different
asupersync acquire entry points (Semaphore::acquire(cx, n) vs
OwnedSemaphorePermit::acquire(Arc<Semaphore>, cx, n)), so an
asupersync-side refactor that only updates one of the two could
silently break the other.

Gated on feature = "asupersync-runtime" like the other cx-aware tests
in this module.
